secondary_archetype: Payments / Card Network Compliance Program Manager

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in the payments industry
Building a function from the ground up implies senior ownership
Ability to thrive with minimal guidance, navigating uncertainty and ambiguity

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in the payments industry, with significant expertise in working with card networks and understanding card network rules and compliance activities.
In-depth understanding of acquiring platforms and systems, and their interactions with card network products and processes.

Proficient in navigating and using JIRA for project management and tracking. Familiarity with card network portals for managing and monitoring compliance activities. Comfortable utilizing Google Workspace tools (such as Sheets, Docs, and Slides) for collaboration and documentation.
Proven track record of effectively collaborating with product and engineering teams to develop new processes and strategies in partnership with them.
Strong program and project management abilities, with the capacity to engage and influence stakeholders outside of direct control.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills to effectively engage with both internal and external partners and drive change.
Ability to thrive with minimal guidance, navigating uncertainty and ambiguity while shaping a new program and function at Stripe.
Strategic thinker adept at identifying cross-functional issues, providing innovative solutions, and communicating the implications of these solutions across existing processes, systems, and teams.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Prior experience at a payment service provider (PSP), card network, or high-growth technology company  is preferred.
Bachelor's degree in a relevant field (e.g., Finance, Technology) is preferred.

scope.surface: Card Network Office (CNO) — new compliance and release management function within Global Partner Engineering and Operations
primary_metrics_inferred: Card network compliance rate / audit outcomes
Mandate intake and prioritization cycle times
core_responsibilities: Establish a release management process for card network mandates
Review and analyze biannual card network releases to identify key business impacts
Monitor compliance with card network requirements and internal policies
Create and maintain JIRA dashboards for mandate tracking
Manage the network mandate intake process and waiver requests
Engage with external network partners in councils, forums, and strategic meetings
Advocate for Stripe within the card network ecosystem

green_flags: Opportunity to build a function from the ground up — high ownership and visibility
Clear articulation of responsibilities with specific deliverables (dashboards, frameworks, release processes)
Cross-functional scope spanning engineering, product, compliance, and external partners
Explicit encouragement to apply if minimum requirements are met, without needing preferred qualifications
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy specified — unclear where this role is based
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned
Building from scratch with 'minimal guidance' may signal limited organizational support or unclear resourcing

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement and scope of building a new function)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
ambiguities: Location type is entirely unstated — no city, remote/hybrid/onsite policy, or work authorization mentioned anywhere in the JD.
The bullet point under minimum requirements contains only a non-breaking space (' '), suggesting a formatting artifact in the source JD rather than a missing requirement.
validation_issues: nice_to_have psp_or_network_employer: raw_text_from_jd not verbatim in JD
nice_to_have bachelor_degree: raw_text_from_jd not verbatim in JD
